title: Tephrosia diversifolia
intro: Tephrosia diversifolia is a perennial legume plant native to tropical regions, known for its medicinal properties and traditional use in herbal remedies. The plant has been reported to exhibit various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Tephrosia diversifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a paste or powder, to treat various ailments, and is also used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Tephrosia diversifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Tephrosia and is native to certain regions. The classification of Tephrosia diversifolia is further supported by its classification within the subfamily Faboideae. This subfamily comprises a large number of legume species. Tephrosia diversifolia's classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological roles.
uses: Tephrosia diversifolia is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat fever, inflammation, and other health issues. It is also used to treat snake and spider bites due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antivenom properties. In some cultures, the plant is used as an antidote for certain types of poisoning. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and ensure its safe use.

side_effects: Tephrosia diversifolia, also known as the "wild pea" or "gungur", is a plant species native to tropical Africa. However, there is limited information available on its side effects.
